title: La Nouvelle France
creator: Marquis de Rays / colonial enterprise
notes: Years: 1879-1885 (Gallica digitized run) | Access: digital archive | Public domain: likely | Recruitment and propaganda newspaper for the colony. | ISSUE COMPLETENESS (audit 2026-07-13): the census (data/census/PB-P001-la-nouvelle-france.json, from Gallica periodical ark cb328261098/date, built 2026-07-08) enumerates 78 digitized issues spanning 1879-07-15..1885-10-15, and ALL 78 are acquired (masters in B2) -> acquirable extent COMPLETE, gap 0 vs Gallica. title: Australian newspaper coverage in Trove
creator: various
notes: Years: 1880-1883+ | Access: digital/public domain (Trove) | Public domain: yes (pre-1955 Australian). | IRREDUCIBLE PRESS RESIDUAL (research 2026-07-13, SRCH-0007): not a single periodical but a topical slice of contemporary Australian reportage on the de Rays / Port-Breton / New Italy affair spread across many papers (Sydney Morning Herald, Sydney Mail, The Tasmanian, et al.) -- abundant and digitized in Trove, effectively unbounded and not exhaustively acquirable (analogous to the French-press residual on PB-P004). NO Trove adapter is warranted: there is no repetitive discrete-acquisition target, so the plan's anticipated PB-P005 Trove adapter (T015) is deliberately NOT pulled (research-first: the need is disproven, not deferred). Landmark article exemplars (Trove article ids, all PD) if the corpus later wants discrete evidentiary anchors -- each acquirable MANUALLY (n=1, no adapter/seam): 898535 (1 Jun 1880 'The New Colony at New Ireland'); 13481147 (25 Mar 1881 'Emigrants for Sydney'); 161883566 (16 Apr 1881 'The Marquis de Rays' Victims'); 199526344 (25 Jun 1881 'The New Ireland Fiasco'); 3420684 (3 Aug 1883 'The Marquis de Rays' Expedition'). Distinct from PB-P006 (New Italy Museum's discrete primary holdings: settler photos + writings).

title: New Italy Museum and survivor materials
creator: various
notes: Custodian: New Italy Museum (Pacific Highway, Northern Rivers, NSW) — a volunteer-run community museum of the ~200 Marquis de Rays expedition survivor families. Reclassified from a monograph stub to a source-group: this is a heterogeneous holding, not a single work, and the former 'Public domain: mixed' resolves to distinct rights buckets. PUBLISHABLE (public domain, per-item on acquisition): pre-1955 settler photographs, and pre-1955 / author-dead-70y survivor writings (see the suspected[] acquisition targets). ALREADY CATALOGED elsewhere: the 1879-1881 de Rays promotional matter and 'Nouvelle France' maps the museum holds copies of are the same works as PB-P002 / P007 / P009-P011 — do not re-catalog. NOT OURS TO PUBLISH (museum or third-party copyright): museum exhibit text, website family stories, and modern photographs of the artifacts; the physical artifacts themselves (travelling trunk, Wagga quilt, farming implements, bocce balls, wine presses) carry no copyright as objects, but any photograph of them is the photographer's. The prior record wrongly listed the holding repository as Gallica / BnF (a template leftover) — corrected to the New Italy Museum; repositoryRecords removed, since a source-group holds none (members carry their own on acquisition). Publishing the public-domain buckets requires acquiring discrete, provenance-bearing items from the museum, each with its own per-item rights determination.

creator: Vermont, Henri
notes: Years: 1884 (192 p.) | Access: bibliographic-only, holding/digitization not yet located | Public domain: likely (publication age). A published account of the trial pleadings before the tribunal de la Seine, DISTINCT from PB-P008 (Las Cases' single Cour de Paris appeal pleading): this is the advocate Me Vermont's plaidoiries for the Marquis. Discovered 2026-07-13 (spec 009 US1, work-bundle PB-P004, Gallica/BnF search pass SRCH-0002) via the legal-history databases Criminocorpus (criminocorpus.org/fr/bibliographie/ouvrages/118666/) and the Bibliographie numérique d'histoire du droit (univ-lorraine). NOT found digitized on Gallica -> likely a physical-only BnF/library holding; inventory + per-item rights confirmation pending a located copy before promotion to approved-for-acquisition. Holding search 2026-07-13 (SRCH-0003) confirmed no digitized copy on Gallica / archive.org / HathiTrust / Google Books -- physical-only; not digitally acquirable at present, so it stays discovered.
